title: podman machine start on macOS x64 fails for a while if the machine had just been stopped
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

After stopping the podman machine, I wanted to restart it soon thereafter, and I kept receiving:

`Error: dial unix /var/folders/qh/xw68c7sn2r72tltmnnd2bbnr0000gn/T/podman/qmp_podman-machine-default.sock: connect: no such file or directory`

I searched for similar issues and found suggestions to delete and re-create the machine, but I had a lot of built containers in my machine that I didn't want to rebuild.

While searching the internet for similar issues, I came back after 5 minutes and tried again and it worked.

I can reproduce this consistently. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine stop`
2. `podman machine start`

**Describe the results you received:**

The machine cannot be started for about 5 minutes after stopping.

**Describe the results you expected:**

A more descriptive error why it can't be started and that I should wait 5 minutes.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

`lsof -i tcp:7777` doesn't show anything:

<details>
<summary>~/.config/containers/containers.conf</summary>

```
[containers]
  log_size_max = -1
  pids_limit = 2048
  userns_size = 65536

[engine]
  num_locks = 2048
  active_service = "podman-machine-default-root"
  stop_timeout = 10
  [engine.service_destinations]
    [engine.service_destinations.podman-machine-default]
      uri = "ssh://core@localhost:60221/run/user/501/podman/podman.sock"
      identity = "/Users/kevin/.ssh/podman-machine-default"
    [engine.service_destinations.podman-machine-default-root]
      uri = "ssh://root@localhost:60221/run/podman/podman.sock"
      identity = "/Users/kevin/.ssh/podman-machine-default"

[machine]

[network]

[secrets]

[configmaps]
```

</details>

comments.body: I'm unable to reproduce but my x86 isn't on the most recent MacOS version. Will update my Mac and check again.

@kgibm Still unable to reproduce on latest macOS 12.5, x86, v4.1.1 from brew. I am able to start my podman machine within seconds of stopping it. I'd recommend uninstalling and installing and see if that fixes your problem.

Ok. I'd rather not uninstall and reinstall as the whole goal here is not to lose my built images, so I am fine just waiting the 5 minutes between stop and start. If you'd like to run any sort of traces on my machine during the issue, I'm glad to do that. Otherwise, we can just close this bug.
